channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 5 ways TikTok profoundly changed the culture
item.description: A lot has changed over the past six years. Seemingly every large media company now has a streaming service. An entire global pandemic happened, and continues to happen. We switched presidents, and then switched back.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Hinge will now use AI to grade your dating profile prompts
item.description: Hinge is releasing a new AI-powered coaching feature to help daters improve their profile prompt responses. Prompts, which are icebreakers that can act as conversation starters, are a staple to many dating app profiles.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Americans are using the Chinese Red Note app to express their frustration with the TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court and the U.S. government seem hell-bent on pushing TikTok out of existence in the United States in the next week—at least unless its Chinese-based parent company, ByteDance, accedes to a fire sale to a suitable buyer based closer to home.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: The 2025 BAFTA nominees have been revealed, and Conclave is the big winner – but one of my favorite movies got nothing, and it's a crime
item.description: The official 2025 BAFTA nominations have been announced, but I'm not impressed that one of 2024's best movies was completely overlooked.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia Brings Frame Generation Enhancements to RTX 4000 Series
item.description: Nvidia is bringing Frame Generation enhancements to the RTX 4000 series, making DLSS 4 accessible to all RTX GPUs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Why Lenovo keeps making weird laptops
item.description: Of all the laptops that debut during the CES trade show every January, Lenovo’s tend to be the most interesting. For years now, the company has indulged in public experimentation, launching laptops with foldable displays, dual screens, and secondary e-ink surfaces.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: CHIPS Act: Will $280 Billion Boost US Semiconductor Production?
item.description: A sweeping 2022 law aims to revive US manufacturing of semiconductors, but a report questions its cost-effectiveness and whether it will deliver the best security for taxpayers.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Friendship ended with Overwatch 2 as Marvel Rivals developer promises a new hero every six weeks
item.description: Marvel Rivals is set to receive plenty of balance changes with the launch of Season 1, NetEase confirms.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: The AOL experience is still kicking in 2025
item.description: AOL is alive and well. Decades after its heyday, AOL offers a subscription-based browser called AOL Desktop Gold, priced at $6.99 per month. While no longer tied to dial-up internet, it retains much of the charm of its late-’90s and early-2000s predecessor.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: California pulls back EPA requests for approval to phase out diesel-powered trains and big rigs
item.description: California has withdrawn requests for the EPA to give the state authority to enforce rules limiting emissions from diesel-powered trains and big rigs.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A CSO’s perspective: 8 cyber predictions for 2025
item.description: As we step into 2025, the cyberthreat landscape is once again more dynamic and challenging than the year before. In 2024, we witnessed a remarkable acceleration in cyberattacks of all types, many fueled by advancements in generative AI. For security leaders, the stakes are higher than ever.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Arm may be planning to hike prices, has considered developing its own chips
item.description: Arm’s future company strategy revealed in still-sealed testimony and documents from December 2024's royalty spat with Qualcomm.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: DJI Mavic 4 Pro leaks show it could improve my favorite camera drone with a redesigned triple camera module
item.description: Leaked images seemingly show the rumored DJI Mavic 4 Pro's redesigned bulbous camera module which could mean improved image quality and range of motion.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Smart glasses’ appeal comes into focus at CES 2025
item.description: Smart glasses attracted a lot of attention at last week’s Consumer Electronics Show, with a range of devices on display that combine lightweight frames with functionality such as heads-up displays and AI-powered assistants.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What were the biggest stories from CES? Tune in to our podcast to find out
item.description: We round up our favorite moments from the Las Vegas tech expo, from the big trends coming in 2025 to the 'weird stuff'.
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: How to save your TikTok videos before the U.S. ban
item.description: TikTok’s future in the United States now lies in the hands of the Supreme Court, and the odds don’t look good for the app. The ban is officially slated for January 19, 2025, when the app could vanish from app stores, leaving new users unable to download it.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Opinion: The case for killing the tax credit for electric vehicles
item.description: In today's mature EV market, such credits provide tax cuts for the rich and discourage other green technologies.
item.dc:creator: Veronique de Rugy, The Mercury News

item.title: Google faces more scrutiny as UK watchdog flexes new digital competition powers
item.description: Britain’s competition watchdog says it’s investigating Google’s search ad business, adding to global scrutiny that the U.S. tech giant is facing.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft component retirement frenzy geared to product simplification: Analyst
item.description: A rash of planned feature and application retirements within the Microsoft 365 environment is the result of Microsoft wanting to centralize, modernize, operationalize customer spend, and ultimately economize by simplifying product offerings where it can, an industry analyst said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tech giants join forces to better support Chromium-based browsers
item.description: The Linux Foundation has unveiled a new collaborative organization called Supporters of Chromium-Based Browsers designed to ensure that open-source projects with connections to Chromium get the necessary resources to be successful.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The future of trucking rolls forward in Shell's experimental big rig
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson takes a closer look at Shell's Starship 3.0: A natural gas big rig boosting efficiency 2.5 times, proving greener trucking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yes, you should use AI coding assistants—but not like that
item.description: If you’re exhausted by the constantly changing AI landscape, you’re not alone. In a thoughtful post, Microsoft Research brainiac Victor Dibia captures the “particular kind of fatigue that comes from trying to match the unprecedented pace of AI advancement.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok app a ‘national security risk,' Sen. Mark Kelly says
item.description: “I imagine [there's gonna] be a disruption in the service here, you know, starting on the 19th,” Kelly told CBS News’s Margaret Brennan on “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The top five procurement themes for 2025
item.description: 2025 will see continued focus on supply chain resiliency, often revolving around support for and investment in the domestic industrial base. The post The top five procurement themes for 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: New malware justifies Apple’s locked-down security strategy
item.description: Apple has told us Macs aren’t secure enough and it continues working to improve their security, as it does across all of its platforms. But a newly identified malware attack confirms that third-party developers can sometimes be a weak link in the perimeter.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chinese auto giant wants to make flying cars your next commute option
item.description: An automotive maker in China has developed a flying car that is setting the stage for a new era in how we think about commuting and connectivity in cities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold Jan. 19 TikTok shutdown
item.description: The case asks the high court to weigh the First Amendment against serious national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: PowerSchool hack exposes Indiana students' data
item.description: BROWNSBURG, Ind. -- Multiple Indianapolis area school corporations confirmed an unauthorized user accessed its student information system as part of a national data breach.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Haughn, NewsNation

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
